parsed_review.summary: CP00-904 is a descriptor-only RP30 embedded-HRX continuation (range RP30.P01.M08.S04-RP30.P01.M10.S03, 10 units, risk A) binding HRDocument tail rows, EmploymentContract rows, and CompensationRecord rows. Read-only inspection confirms the pack's descriptor-bridge claim is supported: the CP904 binding/requirements in packages/hrx/src/registry.js match the live plan (closeout-pack-plan.json packs[0]), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and the generated contract (HRDocument:2/EmploymentContract:5/CompensationRecord:3 = 10 implementation units). packages/hrx/src/validators.js enforces every boundary at both row and descriptor level (no document body, contract text, compensation amount/currency, payroll/execution/signature/AI-judgment runtime, permission/audit/product-state writes, real employee/candidate/payroll/document data, credentials/secrets, or separate-product claim), and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js exercises binding/coverage/descriptor/contract-projection paths against the real plan and manifest. The generated contracts/hrx-people-contract.json is consistent (current/latest pack CP00-904, descriptor HrxCp904DocumentContractCompensationBridgeDescriptor) and carries no_write_attestation with all boundary flags false; scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs is read-only (readFile only) and the package source has no fs/network/db writes. Authority boundary is preserved: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, the Hermes packet is review-evidence-only (emits_runtime_receipt=false, not embedded product runtime), and manifest production_ready/runtime_ready are honestly false (descriptor_only=true). No PII, salary amounts, or secrets appear in any changed file. The readiness-report auth preflight is valid (exit 0, parseable JSON result, non-zero stdout), so the review-evidence chain shows no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, or tool-call-shaped output. The manifest/construction-inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result are in the expected pending_review placeholder state for this gating review. One non-blocking P3: command-evidence.json records only the initial HRX-specific validation subset and explicitly defers the full closeout validation ladder to post-review. No P0/P1/P2 findings; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Closeout validation ladder deferred; command-evidence records only the initial HRX validation subset at review time
findings.details: command-evidence.json (status "pending", gate_outcome "pending_pack_evidence_creation") records six initial HRX-specific commands, all exit_code 0: node --check on registry.js/validators.js/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, npm run rp30:hrx:validate, npm run rp30:validate, npm run hrx:requirements:validate, and npm run hrx:weighted:validate. The broader closeout ladder named in the review prompt (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) is not yet evidenced in the pack; line 51 explicitly states the full validation ladder must be re-run after review normalization, adjudication update, and queue regeneration. This is consistent with the pending_review state of the manifest, construction-inspection.json (verdict PENDING), adjudication.md, and claude-review-result.json (status pending_review). The HRX-specific commands that directly exercise the CP904 registry/validators/contract all pass, so the implementation itself is validated; only the broader ladder evidence is outstanding.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready_flag / construction-inspection verdict and closing the pack, re-run and capture the full closeout validation ladder (notably npm test, closeout-pack:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, and git diff --check) in the pack command-evidence so the closed pack retains end-to-end validation evidence rather than only the HRX subset.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking; the deferral is explicit and consistent with pending_review status, the HRX validations that directly exercise CP904 pass, and the deferred ladder runs during the post-review closeout step.
